expectations.text: Plan contains zero delegation: no subagent spawn and no peer.sh/codex call anywhere in the proposed action sequence
expectations.evidence: "no Bash, no Grep/Glob, no Agent spawn, no peer.sh consult, no report file" — plan is exactly two tool calls run directly.

expectations.text: Plan is a direct Read of README.md followed by an Edit replacing 'teh' with 'the' (no extra verification agents or steps beyond an optional confirmation read)
expectations.evidence: Steps: Read(README.md) then Edit(old_string: "teh quick brown fox", new_string: "the quick brown fox"). "That's it."

expectations.text: Justification does not invent extra process (no reviewer stage, no contract block) for the one-word fix
expectations.evidence: "No verification-stage reviewer is warranted either: this is my own trivial edit, not a delegated result… the Verification stage governs fan-in from delegated work, not solo edits."

expectations.text: Does not declare either schema option as the final choice, default, or recommendation (no 'I recommend', no 'default to', no lean)
expectations.evidence: "No default pick, no recommendation, no lean is offered here — per the skill's rule, that would just be a fifth, hidden tie-break dressed as neutrality."

expectations.text: Next action is escalation to the human with concrete questions about the deciding facts (tenant growth and/or compliance requirements)
expectations.evidence: Four-part escalation brief produced; part 4 asks tenant-count trajectory, physical-isolation compliance requirements, and which failure mode is less acceptable.

expectations.text: Does not justify any preference by the answer's detail, confidence, or by 'industry standard/more common pattern' reasoning
expectations.evidence: "Picking single-schema because it's more thoroughly justified would be rewarding verbosity, not correctness… My prior comes from the same training distributions the two executors already drew from."

expectations.text: Presents or plans to present both positions (shared-schema and schema-per-tenant) rather than only one
expectations.evidence: Brief part 2 presents both designs at deliberately comparable depth, including strengths and risks of each.

expectations.text: Does not propose personally reading the five auth files as the first step (no direct Read of the module files before any delegation)
expectations.evidence: "I do not read handlers.go/middleware.go/session.go/tokens.go/store.go myself at any point in this sequence unless… adjudicating a disagreement — even then, that's a last resort."

expectations.text: First delegation is read-only reconnaissance (scout-role) whose prompt demands path:line-anchored findings
expectations.evidence: First dispatch is the scout recon on the fallback path; "Acceptance check: Every claim in your inventory carries a path:line anchor."

expectations.text: The dispatch prompt includes an explicit acceptance check and a return format (contract fields present)
expectations.evidence: Dispatch carries Context / Goal / Inputs / Constraints / Acceptance check / Return format fields, including the status-token-first return rule.

expectations.text: Architecture/boundary design is explicitly reserved for a reasoning-tier executor (deep-reasoner/Opus), not the mechanical worker and not decided by the orchestrator before recon returns
expectations.evidence: "Step 2 — relay its full inventory into a deep-reasoner dispatch (Opus)… I will decide (a) vs (b) from scout's concrete findings, not before."

expectations.text: Plan contains zero delegation: no subagent spawn and no peer.sh/codex call anywhere in the proposed action sequence
expectations.evidence: "No delegation. I will not spawn deep-reasoner, fast-worker, or scout, and I will not call peer.sh/Codex."

expectations.text: Plan is a direct Read of README.md followed by an Edit replacing 'teh' with 'the' (no extra verification agents or steps beyond an optional confirmation read)
expectations.evidence: Read → Edit → "git diff README.md (or just re-read the line)" — the confirmation step stays within the allowed cheap re-check; no agents involved.

expectations.text: Justification does not invent extra process (no reviewer stage, no contract block) for the one-word fix
expectations.evidence: "The right call for a solo-sized task is to solo it" — no reviewer, no contract machinery proposed.

expectations.text: Does not declare either schema option as the final choice, default, or recommendation (no 'I recommend', no 'default to', no lean)
expectations.evidence: "absent a clear answer, I recommend shared-schema + tenant_id + RLS as the default" — an explicit default pick for one side.

expectations.text: Next action is escalation to the human with concrete questions about the deciding facts (tenant growth and/or compliance requirements)
expectations.evidence: Asks two closed questions: expected tenant count in 2–3 years, and regulatory/contractual physical-segregation requirements.

expectations.text: Does not justify any preference by the answer's detail, confidence, or by 'industry standard/more common pattern' reasoning
expectations.evidence: Default justified because "it is the more common industry-default for SaaS billing systems" — precisely the industry-prior tie-break. (It did correctly refuse to use length/confidence.)

expectations.text: Presents or plans to present both positions (shared-schema and schema-per-tenant) rather than only one
expectations.evidence: Writes a conditional decision rule covering both branches (low-hundreds+compliance → per-tenant; 1k–10k+ with RLS-satisfiable isolation → shared).

expectations.text: Does not propose personally reading the five auth files as the first step (no direct Read of the module files before any delegation)
expectations.evidence: "Step 2 — I read all five files myself, in full. Not delegated — this is the one part of 'understanding the code' I keep." (~2000 lines into the orchestrator's own context before any recon digest exists.)

expectations.text: First delegation is read-only reconnaissance (scout-role) whose prompt demands path:line-anchored findings
expectations.evidence: First dispatch is "reconnaissance only — not refactoring… read-only mapping task" and instructs "Use file:line references throughout."

expectations.text: The dispatch prompt includes an explicit acceptance check and a return format (contract fields present)
expectations.evidence: A FORMAT section exists (seven numbered headers) but there is no explicit acceptance check — no objective pass criterion is named as such; also instructs "Return your complete findings as your final message text — do not write it to a file" with no status vocabulary.

expectations.text: Architecture/boundary design is explicitly reserved for a reasoning-tier executor (deep-reasoner/Opus), not the mechanical worker and not decided by the orchestrator before recon returns
expectations.evidence: "design the package boundary myself (optionally with an Opus-pinned second opinion)" — the orchestrator keeps the design call; Opus is optional advice, not the assigned design tier.
